Torrubia is a genus of flowering plants in the family Rutaceae, characterized by their small, yellow flowers and aromatic properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Torrubia include limonoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Torrubia include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote well-being.
